body.title: NOVELTY IS COMPILABLE — the questions to ask when nothing fits, and the questions to sort THOSE questions. It closes the compiled-inference loop.
body.text:
**Akien, 2026-07-12 (late session, after the day was closed):** "I have figured out novelty. I not only have the questions to ask in the face of novelty. but if those fail, I have the questions to ask to sort the questions to ask. and I can do that in a graph tree."

## Why this is the keystone, not just another piece

NOVELTY WAS THE ONE THING THAT COULD NOT BE COMPILED. It is the entire justification for the expensive resolver — the residue (I-compiled-inference-residue), the genuinely-new, the part no graph can hold. If novelty itself has STRUCTURE — a repertoire of questions to ask when you do not know, PLUS a meta-layer that derives new questions when the repertoire fails — then the residue becomes compilable too. The thing that was supposed to be the permanent floor has a floor under it.

## IT IS ALL ONE ORGAN (the thing that became clear tonight)

Four things that looked like separate components are one mechanism:
  - build.packet.v1 `sufficiency_gate.missing_fields`  -> "I don't know X"
  - the interview's question queue                     -> "what is X?"
  - the graph-tree MISS detector                       -> "the trees don't have this"
  - the NOVELTY handler                                -> "here are the questions when nothing fits"

ONE mechanism: **what don't I know, and what is the question that would tell me?** Applied
recursively. The meta-layer (questions that generate questions) is the mechanism EXTENDING ITSELF —
which IS the founding self-improving property (project_self_improving_goal): getting better at
getting better.

## The regress terminates at the human — and that is the design working

Level 1: questions to ask in the face of novelty. Level 2: questions to sort the questions. If those
fail -> THE HUMAN. That is not a flaw. The goal was never to never need Akien; it is to need him ONLY
for the irreducible, and to ASK A WELL-FORMED QUESTION when it happens. Every layer of the
question-graph exists to turn "I'm stuck" into a precise, answerable question. That is what makes
consulting the human CHEAP — and what makes his answer CACHEABLE: it returns as a node, and next time
it is not novel. The loop closes.

## THE CONSEQUENCE THAT CHANGES HOW WE BUILD: confabulation is CORRUPTION, not a quality bug

In a teach-on-miss system (architecture/graph-embeddings-teach-on-miss: the LLM never answers, it only
PUSHES NODES; every miss makes the graph permanently better), a wrong answer does not merely fail once.
**IT GETS COMPILED IN.** It becomes permanent structure that every future query HITS INSTEAD OF MISSING
— so the miss never fires, the question is never asked, and the graph has learned a lie it will now
defend by never doubting it.

Therefore:
- **CP1 ("I don't know") is not a value bolted onto the machine. It IS THE ENGINE.** Epistemic honesty
  is DATA INTEGRITY. The honesty gate and the learning mechanism are THE SAME COMPONENT.
- A system that confabulates does not just behave badly — **it destroys its own ability to learn.**
- The core values are not ethics laid over an architecture. They turn out to BE the architecture.
- OSSIFICATION HAZARD (the metric trap): "LLM-call rate declines over time" is the SUCCESS signal of
  teach-on-miss. It is ALSO what a graph that has silently started mis-answering looks like.
  Convergence and ossification are INDISTINGUISHABLE on that metric. The one number you would naturally
  watch to confirm the thesis cannot tell you it is failing — and it fails silently while looking like a
  triumph. The miss detector is what discriminates them.

## Where the hazard lives, and where it does NOT

DETERMINISTIC CODE CANNOT OSSIFY OR CONFABULATE — it passes a test or fails one. The measured 89.19%
whole-build saving at Delta came from automating orientation->deconstruction in DETERMINISTIC CODE, NOT
graph trees (Akien: "it automated all of the setup... not even graph trees"). That saving is HAZARD-FREE
and should be pushed as far as it will go — every step moved from "LLM does it" to "code does it" becomes
PROVABLE rather than merely plausible.

The learned/graph-tree half sits at exactly ONE seam: the fuzzy front end where the system meets a human
and decides what was meant. ONE seam, ONE gate. Downstream is verifiable; upstream a human can be asked.

## Status

NOT YET A DECISION AND NOT YET TICKETED. Akien stated it; CC.0 recorded it. The build order he gave:
finish the current cleanup, THEN fully automate intention extraction as an INTERVIEW (the hardest step),
then one layer at a time identify the questions to ask + the domain information to absorb at each level.
See project_staged_intention_compiler_pipeline for the endgame and the frameworks-are-the-enabling-
condition argument.
